subject: FINDING: neither Fivetran Claude connector works for a personal claude.ai account
description: VERIFIED from Fivetran docs: Platform connector needs a Console Admin API key (platform.claude.com); Compliance connector needs a Claude ENTERPRISE account with the Compliance API enabled by an Anthropic rep. Operator uses ONLY personal claude.ai (no Admin key, no Enterprise). Therefore the data/models/fivetran schema is correct as a MODEL but has NO live ingestion path via Fivetran for this operator. Real capture path = claude.ai self-serve data export (Settings > Account > Export data) -> conversations.json/projects.json/users.json.

subject: Build claude.ai export -> compliance/* loader (the actual ingestion)
description: NOT DONE. The 23 compliance/ tables exist as schema only. Need a loader that reads a claude.ai data-export ZIP (conversations.json, projects.json, users.json) and UPSERTs into claude_ai.* tables deterministically (PK upsert, idempotent). Target prototype: Cloudflare Worker + D1 or Postgres. This is the 'actually store data' step.

note: CORRECTION (operator-flagged): the Fivetran-specific finding stands (Platform=Admin key, Compliance=Enterprise), BUT 'no live ingestion path' was too strong. Claude Desktop > Developer > Configure Third-Party Inference routes ALL desktop inference through a Gateway base URL the operator controls, with custom headers + credential kind. A gateway the operator runs IS a live capture point: it sees every inference request+response. This is a valid go-forward capture lane with no Admin/Enterprise dep.

subject: Build inference-gateway logger Worker (capture lane via Configure Third-Party Inference)
description: Claude Desktop > Developer > Configure Third-Party Inference lets the operator set a Gateway base URL + custom headers + credential kind. Build a Cloudflare Worker that (a) accepts the Anthropic-shaped inference requests from Claude Desktop at that base URL, (b) logs prompt+response (and token usage) to D1/Postgres, (c) forwards upstream to the real provider and streams the response back unchanged. This captures the operator's go-forward Claude Desktop conversation data with NO Fivetran/Admin/Enterprise dependency, and IS the Cloudflare prototype the operator wanted. Maps captured rows onto claude_ai.chat/chat_message/chat_message_content where possible.

pass_if: Claude Desktop set to Gateway base URL = the Worker; a chat round-trips correctly
Worker logs each request+response to a store (D1 or Postgres)
forwarded responses are byte-identical to upstream (streaming preserved)
captured rows map to claude_ai.* schema
fail_if: gateway alters or drops response content
credential/billing behavior asserted without verification

subject: VERIFY: how gateway mode interacts with the Max subscription (auth + billing)
description: OPEN/UNVERIFIED. When Configure Third-Party Inference is set, requests go where the gateway forwards. It is NOT established that the gateway can forward subscription-authenticated traffic to Anthropic and keep using the Max plan (vs. requiring an API key, or the gateway pointing at a local/3rd-party model). The 'Credential kind' field selects the source with NO fallback. Do not claim subscription passthrough works until tested end-to-end. Also: this captures Claude DESKTOP traffic only, not claude.ai web/mobile chats (those still need the data export).

pass_if: tested whether gateway can use the desktop subscription credential to reach real Claude
documented what model actually answers and how it's billed
fail_if: subscription passthrough claimed without a test

subject: Make the gateway logger PROVIDER-AGNOSTIC on the OpenAI chat/completions format
description: VERIFIED (platform.claude.com/.../openai-sdk): OpenAI /v1/chat/completions is the interop standard; Anthropic exposes it at api.anthropic.com/v1/, and Docker Model Runner, Cloudflare Workers AI, Vertex, Azure, Bedrock all speak it. Design the Worker to normalize on that format so it fronts ANY of them and captures uniformly (usage.prompt_tokens/completion_tokens are returned). This removes the Anthropic-API-key dependency: route to Workers AI (operator CF account) or local Docker Model Runner instead. Caveat: Anthropic's compat layer is 'test not production' and silently ignores response_format/seed/prompt-caching/strict.

pass_if: Worker captures normalized chat/completions from >=2 providers (e.g. Workers AI + Docker Model Runner)
token usage logged from usage.* fields
no Anthropic API key required for the non-Anthropic providers
fail_if: design hard-codes the Anthropic Messages format
capture breaks when provider changes

subject: SPIKE: on-device capture lane (Apple Foundation Models + Chrome Gemma/Nano) — NOT gateway-frontable
description: VERIFIED (apple-foundation-models doc): Apple FM runs the model with Apple NOT in the request path; ClaudeForFM talks native Messages API direct app->Anthropic. Chrome built-in AI (Gemini Nano) and Gemma in Chrome/Canary run on-device in the browser. NONE of these flow through an HTTP gateway, so the Worker cannot capture them. If the operator wants these captured, it must be done IN-APP at the SDK level (Swift FoundationModels transcript; JS Prompt API). Chrome's exact built-in-AI capture API is UNVERIFIED - spike it, do not assume. Relevant to the coworkers Swift app: it can swap Apple on-device model <-> Claude per LanguageModelSession.

pass_if: documented that on-device models bypass the HTTP gateway
decided whether to capture them in-app
Chrome built-in-AI API verified by a real probe, not assumed
fail_if: on-device lane conflated with the HTTP gateway
Chrome API claimed without a test

note: SUPERSEDED by v1 (open-managed-agents). The inference gateway already EXISTS: packages/server/src/providers/index.ts (Vercel AI SDK, 7 providers incl. any OpenAI-compatible + Ollama, uniform {content,stop_reason,usage}); middleware/anthropic.ts is the 1:1 Anthropic-compatible API (reads x-api-key) = the Configure-Third-Party-Inference target; packages/worker-api is a DEPLOYED Cloudflare Worker on subagentworkspaces.com/v1/* (D1+KV+vaults+provider rotation, CF account e6294e3e = Alex@jadecli). Capture lands in fact.fact_llm_request / events.events_llm_token / streams.streams_llm_tokens via the Rust data-engineer collect_events workflow. Do NOT rebuild.
SUPERSEDED by v1. Provider-agnostic OpenAI-format normalization already shipped in providers/index.ts (@ai-sdk/openai-compatible) and dim_provider. No rebuild needed.

subject: FINDING: v1 (open-managed-agents) already implements the gateway+Worker+warehouse+format I was proposing
description: Reviewed the uploaded v1 zip. It already has: (1) a provider-agnostic LLM gateway (packages/server providers, 1:1 Anthropic API), (2) a deployed Cloudflare Worker (packages/worker-api, D1, subagentworkspaces.com), (3) a Kimball capture warehouse (Rust data-engineer: dim/fact/cube/events/streams, fact_llm_request, Cube.dev semantic layer, pg_durable, Redis streams), and (4) THE one-file YAML+SQL format I was told to build — it is the '-- ===cube=== / -- ===sql===' convention parsed by src/schema/cube_yaml.rs. My proposal v1/v2 re-proposed shipped work. Owning that. Pivot to the real, narrow delta below.

pass_if: operator agrees the gateway/Worker/warehouse already exist
work pivots to the delta
fail_if: effort spent rebuilding existing v1 capability

subject: DELTA-1: reconcile data/models/fivetran/*.yaml to the v1 house format (===cube===/===sql===)
description: My 40 Fivetran files use an invented 'kind: FivetranTableSemantics' YAML with ddl:|. The actual house format (cube_yaml.rs) is a .sql file with a '-- ===cube=== ... -- ===sql===' header: version, table_type (dim|fact|cube|events|streams), a Cube.dev cube{measures,dimensions,joins,pre_aggregations}, then SQL DDL below. Convert the claude.ai compliance tables to this format so they're consistent with the data-engineer warehouse and parseable by cube_yaml.rs. Retire the invented YAML.

pass_if: claude.ai tables emitted as ===cube===/===sql=== .sql files
cube_yaml.rs can parse them
no invented FivetranTableSemantics format remains
fail_if: two competing table-definition formats persist in the repo

subject: DELTA-2: the ONLY genuinely new capability — claude.ai conversation CONTENT capture
description: v1 captures LLM TELEMETRY (tokens/latency/cost per model request) from OMA agents via collect_events. It does NOT capture personal claude.ai CONVERSATION CONTENT (chats/messages/projects/artifacts) — different grain, and OMA never sees your claude.ai web/Desktop usage. This is the real additive piece. Source = claude.ai Settings>Export data (conversations.json/projects.json). Model it as events_* + dim_* tables in the v1 format and write a loader. Separately: pointing Claude Desktop's Configure-Third-Party-Inference at the EXISTING worker-api gateway (subagentworkspaces.com/v1) would route+capture Desktop telemetry into fact_llm_request — verify, incl. the still-open Max-subscription-auth question (task 3a417c70).

pass_if: conversation-content tables exist in v1 format
export loader upserts conversations.json idempotently
decision recorded on routing Claude Desktop through the existing worker-api gateway
fail_if: conversation content conflated with the existing telemetry tables

note: CORRECTION (verified from support.claude.com Cowork architecture doc): Cowork activity is NOT captured in audit logs, the Compliance API, OR data exports. So the claude.ai data-export path proposed here does NOT capture Cowork. The OFFICIAL + only supported capture lane is OpenTelemetry ('Monitor Cowork activity with OpenTelemetry'). Conversation CONTENT export still has value for web/mobile chats, but for Cowork/Claude Code activity the path is OTel. See new task OTEL-1.

FURTHER SUPERSEDED: Claude Code/Cowork already EMIT telemetry via OTel (CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1). No wire-proxy gateway is needed to capture their inference - just point the OTel exporter at a collector. Gateway only matters for non-OTel-emitting inference. Use OTEL-1 instead.

subject: OTEL-1: capture Cowork/Claude Code activity via OpenTelemetry into the v1 warehouse
description: VERIFIED (code.claude.com/monitoring-usage). Enable on desktop: CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1, OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER=otlp, OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER=otlp, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL=grpc, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://<collector>:4317, OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS=Authorization=Bearer... . Schema maps ~1:1 to v1 fact.fact_llm_request: span claude_code.llm_request {model, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_tokens} -> fact_llm_request cols; metrics claude_code.{cost.usage,token.usage(type=input|output|cacheRead|cacheCreation),session.count,commit.count,pull_request.count,lines_of_code.count} -> the claude_code_usage_report core_metrics_* columns already built. Path: desktop OTel -> OTLP collector -> Postgres bridge -> fact_llm_request / streams_llm_tokens (reuse data-engineer collect_events). Stand up an OTLP->Postgres collector (or otel-collector config already drafted in apps/analytics-dashboard/cost).

pass_if: desktop OTel export reaches a collector
a claude_code.api_request event lands in fact_llm_request
token.usage metric rows map to the right columns
fail_if: capture attempted via data export (does not include Cowork)
schema invented instead of reusing v1
